prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: Explain how neural networks work.
answer: Neural networks implement parameterized function approximation f(x; θ) through layered compositions of affine transformations and nonlinear activations. For an L-layer network: h_l = σ(W_l · h_{l-1} + b_l), where W_l ∈ R^{d_l × d_{l-1}}. Training minimizes loss L(θ) via SGD: θ_{t+1} = θ_t - η∇L(θ_t). Gradients computed via backpropagation (chain rule applied layer-by-layer). Common architectures: MLPs (dense), CNNs (conv2d kernels, typically 3×3), Transformers (self-attention, O(n²d) complexity). Activation functions: ReLU (max(0,x)), GELU, SiLU. Regularization: dropout (p=0.1-0.5), weight decay (λ=1e-4), batch normalization.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: What is climate change and why does it matter?
answer: Climate change: statistically significant deviation in mean climate variables over 30+ year periods. Current driver: anthropogenic CO2 emissions at ~36.8 GtCO2/yr (2023). Atmospheric CO2: ~421 ppm (pre-industrial: ~280 ppm). Radiative forcing: +2.72 W/m² (2011-2019 avg). Observable impacts: +1.1°C global mean surface temp (GMST) since 1850-1900 baseline. Sea level rise: 3.7 mm/yr (2006-2018). Arctic sea ice declining ~13%/decade. IPCC AR6 projects 1.5°C threshold breach by early 2030s under SSP2-4.5. Mitigation target: net-zero CO2 by 2050 to limit warming to 1.5°C.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: Describe evolution by natural selection.
answer: Natural selection operates on heritable phenotypic variation under differential fitness. Formally: Δp = p·q·[p(w_AA - w_Aa) + q(w_Aa - w_aa)] / w̄, where p,q are allele frequencies, w is fitness, w̄ is mean population fitness. Requirements: (1) variation exists, (2) variation is heritable (h² > 0), (3) differential survival/reproduction. Mechanisms: directional selection (shifts mean), stabilizing (reduces variance), disruptive (increases variance). Molecular evidence: dN/dS ratio < 1 indicates purifying selection, > 1 positive selection. Speciation typically requires reproductive isolation + ~10^5-10^6 generations for significant divergence.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: Explain supply and demand.
answer: Supply function: Q_s = f(P, C, T) where P=price, C=input costs, T=technology. Demand function: Q_d = g(P, I, P_sub, P_comp) where I=income, P_sub=substitute prices. Equilibrium: Q_s = Q_d at price P*. Price elasticity of demand: E_d = (ΔQ_d/Q_d)/(ΔP/P). |E_d| > 1: elastic, |E_d| < 1: inelastic. Consumer surplus = ∫[P* to P_max] D(P)dP. Producer surplus = ∫[P_min to P*] S(P)dP. Market efficiency maximized when total surplus is maximized (no deadweight loss). Shifts: demand curve shifts right with income increase (normal goods), supply shifts right with cost reduction.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: What is quantum computing?
answer: Quantum computing operates on qubits: |ψ⟩ = α|0⟩ + β|1⟩, where |α|² + |β|² = 1. Key principles: superposition (qubit exists in multiple states), entanglement (CNOT gate creates Bell states), interference (constructive/destructive amplitude manipulation). Gate model: single-qubit (Hadamard, Pauli-X/Y/Z, phase) + two-qubit (CNOT) gates form universal set. Complexity: BQP ⊇ P, relationship to NP unknown. Shor's algorithm: O((log N)³) for factoring (vs O(exp(n^{1/3})) classical). Grover's: O(√N) unstructured search. Current hardware: superconducting (IBM, 1121 qubits), trapped ion (IonQ, ~32 qubits), photonic. Error rates: ~10^{-3} per gate. Fault tolerance requires ~1000:1 physical:logical qubit ratio.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: How does the immune system protect the body?
answer: Immune defense operates in two tiers. Innate: pattern recognition receptors (TLRs, NLRs) detect PAMPs (e.g., LPS, dsRNA). Response: phagocytosis (neutrophils: t½ ~5hr, macrophages), complement cascade (C3 convertase → MAC), NK cells (perforin/granzyme pathway). Adaptive: TCR-MHC interaction drives T-cell activation. CD8+ cytotoxic T-cells: MHC-I restricted, kill infected cells via Fas/FasL and perforin. CD4+ helper T-cells: Th1 (IFN-γ, cell-mediated), Th2 (IL-4/IL-13, humoral). B-cells: V(D)J recombination generates ~10^11 unique BCRs. Affinity maturation via somatic hypermutation in germinal centers. Memory cells: half-life of years to decades. IgG response: primary ~10-14 days, secondary ~3-5 days.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: Explain the theory of relativity.
answer: Special relativity (SR): postulates invariance of physics in inertial frames and c = 299,792,458 m/s in vacuum. Lorentz transformation: t' = γ(t - vx/c²), x' = γ(x - vt), γ = 1/√(1 - v²/c²). Time dilation: Δt = γΔt₀. Length contraction: L = L₀/γ. Mass-energy: E² = (pc)² + (mc²)². General relativity (GR): Einstein field equations G_μν + Λg_μν = (8πG/c⁴)T_μν. Geodesic equation describes free-fall. Schwarzschild radius: r_s = 2GM/c². Predictions confirmed: gravitational lensing, frame-dragging (Gravity Probe B: 37.2 ± 7.2 mas/yr), gravitational waves (LIGO, 2015).

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: What is blockchain technology?
answer: Blockchain: append-only Merkle tree-linked list. Each block B_n contains: header (prev_hash, timestamp, nonce, merkle_root), transaction set T. Hash function: SHA-256 (Bitcoin), Keccak-256 (Ethereum). Consensus: PoW requires finding nonce s.t. H(header) < target (difficulty adjusts every 2016 blocks in BTC). PoS: validator selection weighted by stake, slashing for equivocation. Transaction throughput: BTC ~7 TPS, ETH ~15-30 TPS, Solana ~65,000 TPS (theoretical). Smart contracts: Turing-complete EVM bytecode, gas metering prevents halting problem. Block time: BTC ~10min, ETH ~12s. Finality: probabilistic (PoW, 6 confirmations ~60min) vs deterministic (BFT-based).

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: How does machine learning differ from traditional programming?
answer: Traditional programming: explicit mapping f: X → Y defined by developer-written rules. Deterministic, verifiable, O(1) deployment. ML: parameterized model f(x; θ) where θ learned from data D = {(x_i, y_i)}. Training: θ* = argmin_θ Σ L(f(x_i; θ), y_i) + λR(θ). Key differences: (1) Traditional requires domain expertise for rules; ML requires data + compute. (2) Traditional: interpretable by construction; ML: often black-box. (3) Traditional: maintenance = code updates; ML: data drift monitoring, retraining pipelines. (4) ML scales to high-dimensional inputs (images: 224×224×3 = 150K features) where manual rules are infeasible. Trade-off: ML needs 10²-10⁶ labeled examples depending on task complexity.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: Explain the water cycle.
answer: Hydrological cycle energy budget: solar input ~340 W/m² drives phase transitions. Evaporation: latent heat flux ~80 W/m², rate governed by Penman-Monteith equation. Global evaporation: ~500,000 km³/yr (86% oceanic). Transpiration: stomatal conductance modulated by VPD, CO2 concentration. Atmospheric water vapor: mean residence time ~10 days, precipitable water ~25 mm. Precipitation mechanisms: orographic lifting, convective, frontal. Global precipitation: ~505,000 km³/yr. Runoff coefficient varies 0.1-0.9 depending on terrain, soil, land cover. Groundwater recharge: ~12,600 km³/yr. Total freshwater: ~35 million km³ (2.5% of total), 69% locked in ice.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: What causes earthquakes?
answer: Earthquakes result from brittle failure along fault planes. Stress accumulation: σ = G·ε (shear modulus × strain). Failure criterion: Coulomb stress τ ≥ τ_0 + μ(σ_n - P_f), where μ = friction coefficient (~0.6-0.85), P_f = pore fluid pressure. Seismic moment: M_0 = μ·A·D (rigidity × area × displacement). Moment magnitude: M_w = (2/3)(log₁₀M_0 - 9.1). P-waves: 5-8 km/s (compressional), S-waves: 3-4.5 km/s (shear). Gutenberg-Richter law: log₁₀N = a - bM (b ≈ 1.0). Fault types: normal (extensional), reverse/thrust (compressional), strike-slip (transform). Largest recorded: 1960 Chile M_w 9.5.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: How does encryption keep data secure?
answer: Symmetric encryption: AES-256 operates on 128-bit blocks, 14 rounds of SubBytes/ShiftRows/MixColumns/AddRoundKey. Key space: 2^256. Throughput: ~1-4 GB/s (AES-NI). Asymmetric: RSA security relies on integer factorization hardness. Key generation: p,q primes, n=pq, e·d ≡ 1 (mod φ(n)). RSA-2048: ~112-bit security. ECC: secp256k1 (Bitcoin), Curve25519 (modern TLS). ECDH key exchange + AES-GCM (authenticated encryption) is standard TLS 1.3 cipher suite. Hash functions: SHA-256 (collision resistance: birthday bound 2^128). HKDF for key derivation. Post-quantum: CRYSTALS-Kyber (NIST PQC standard), lattice-based, ~768-1024 dimensional.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: Explain compound interest.
answer: Compound interest: A = P(1 + r/n)^(nt). Variables: P=principal, r=annual rate, n=compounding frequency, t=years. Continuous compounding limit: A = Pe^(rt). Doubling time (Rule of 72): t ≈ 72/r%. Effective annual rate: EAR = (1 + r/n)^n - 1. Example: P=$10,000, r=7%, n=12, t=30yr → A = $10,000(1.00583)^360 = $81,164.97. Real return: r_real ≈ r_nominal - inflation. NPV of future cash flows: NPV = Σ CF_t/(1+r)^t. Compound interest drives exponential growth in wealth accumulation and is fundamental to DCF valuation, bond pricing (YTM), and amortization schedules.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: What is the greenhouse effect?
answer: Greenhouse effect: selective atmospheric absorption of outgoing longwave radiation (OLR). Earth radiates ~240 W/m² (effective temp ~255K). Greenhouse gases absorption bands: CO2 (15μm bending mode), H2O (6.3μm, rotational continuum), CH4 (3.3μm, 7.7μm), O3 (9.6μm). Radiative forcing: ΔF = 5.35·ln(C/C₀) W/m² for CO2. Climate sensitivity: λ = ΔT/ΔF ≈ 0.8 K/(W/m²) without feedbacks. With feedbacks (water vapor, ice-albedo, cloud): ECS = 2.5-4.0°C per CO2 doubling (IPCC AR6). Current anthropogenic forcing: ~2.72 W/m² (2019). Atmospheric window: 8-13μm transmits ~80% of surface radiation directly to space.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: How do vaccines work?
answer: Vaccines exploit adaptive immune memory. Mechanism: antigen presentation via MHC-II on dendritic cells → CD4+ T-cell activation → B-cell activation in germinal centers → affinity maturation (somatic hypermutation, AID enzyme) → class-switch recombination (IgM → IgG) → plasma cells + memory B-cells. Vaccine types: (1) Live-attenuated: replication-competent, strong response, ~95% efficacy (MMR). (2) Inactivated: heat/formaldehyde treated, requires adjuvant. (3) Subunit: purified protein + adjuvant (aluminum salts). (4) mRNA: LNP-encapsulated modified mRNA (N1-methylpseudouridine), translated by ribosomes, spike protein expressed on cell surface. Antibody titers: peak at 2-4 weeks post-vaccination, t½ ~73 days for IgG. Memory B-cell pool stable for years.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: What is artificial intelligence?
answer: AI: computational systems exhibiting intelligent behavior. Taxonomy: (1) Symbolic AI: knowledge representation via first-order logic, expert systems, planning (STRIPS). (2) Connectionist: neural networks, universal approximation theorem. (3) Statistical: Bayesian inference, probabilistic graphical models. Current SOTA: transformer architecture (Vaswani et al., 2017). Self-attention: Attention(Q,K,V) = softmax(QK^T/√d_k)V. Scaling laws: L(N) ∝ N^{-α} (Kaplan et al., 2020), α ≈ 0.076 for language. Training compute: GPT-4 estimated ~10^25 FLOP. Evaluation: MMLU, HumanEval, MATH benchmarks. Alignment: RLHF (PPO), DPO, constitutional AI. Inference optimization: KV-cache, speculative decoding, quantization (INT4/INT8).

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: Explain recursion in programming.
answer: Recursion: function f calls f within its body. Stack frame per call: O(n) space for depth-n recursion. Base case: termination condition preventing infinite recursion. Recurrence relations: T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n), solved via Master theorem. Examples: factorial T(n) = T(n-1) + O(1) → O(n). Merge sort: T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(n) → O(n log n). Fibonacci naive: T(n) = T(n-1) + T(n-2) → O(φ^n). Optimization: memoization reduces to O(n). Tail recursion: f(n, acc) where recursive call is last operation; compiler can optimize to O(1) space (TCO). Stack overflow: default limit ~8MB (Linux), ~1MB (Windows) ≈ 10^4-10^5 frames. Alternative: iterative + explicit stack.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: How does GPS work?
answer: GPS: 31 satellites in 6 orbital planes, altitude 20,180 km, period 11h58m. Each satellite carries Rb/Cs atomic clocks (accuracy ~10^{-13} s). Signal: L1 (1575.42 MHz) C/A code at 1.023 MHz chip rate, L2 (1227.60 MHz) P(Y) code. Pseudorange: ρ = c·Δt + c·δt_r (receiver clock bias). Position fix requires 4 pseudoranges: 3 spatial + 1 clock. Solution via iterative least squares on linearized observation equations. Error sources: ionospheric delay (~5m, corrected via dual-frequency), tropospheric (~0.5m), multipath (~1m), ephemeris (~2m). DGPS/RTK: carrier-phase measurements achieve ~2cm accuracy. Relativistic corrections: +45μs/day (GR) - 7μs/day (SR) = +38μs/day net.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: Apologize for delay and propose meeting times.
answer: Apologies for the response latency. Proposing two time slots for synchronization: (1) Tuesday 10:00 UTC-7 (Pacific), duration 30-60min. (2) Thursday 14:00 UTC-7, same duration. Platform: Zoom/Teams/Meet - specify preference. Agenda items to be shared via shared doc prior to meeting. Please confirm slot availability and I will issue calendar invites with meeting link and dial-in. If neither slot works, share 3 available windows via scheduling tool (Calendly/When2Meet) and I will accommodate.

prompt:
Style: Technical Precise
Task: Summarize benefits of regular exercise.
answer: Exercise physiological benefits: Cardiovascular: VO2max increase ~15-20% with 12-week program, resting HR reduction ~5-10 bpm, LDL reduction ~5-10%. Metabolic: GLUT4 translocation improves insulin sensitivity ~20-40%, RMR increase ~7% with resistance training. Musculoskeletal: bone mineral density +1-3%/yr with weight-bearing exercise, sarcopenia prevention (muscle protein synthesis rate +50-100% post-resistance training). Neurological: BDNF upregulation, hippocampal volume increase ~2% with aerobic exercise (Erickson et al., 2011). Mental health: effect size d=0.4-0.8 for depression reduction. Dose-response: 150 min/wk moderate (3-6 METs) or 75 min/wk vigorous (>6 METs). Mortality risk reduction: ~30-35% all-cause with regular activity.
